meta.title: Florida Rabbit Rescue Directory
meta.verifier_notes: All data sourced from publicly available websites, GuideStar, Charity Navigator, Florida Division of Corporations (Sunbiz), and IRS Business Master File. Mission statements are verbatim or as close to verbatim as publicly available. Founding years sourced from FL corporate records, organization websites, or IRS ruling year where available.

shared_values.intro: The following values are drawn from the published philosophy pages of House Rabbit Society and Rabbit.org Foundation. Both organizations share a deep commitment to rabbit welfare, and their core beliefs overlap substantially.

rorg.name: Rabbit.org Foundation
rorg.note: Independent organization. No affiliation with House Rabbit Society.

values.statement: Rabbits are intelligent, social animals who thrive indoors as companion animals
values.hrs_quote: Help people see rabbits as intelligent and social pets that thrive indoors
values.rorg_quote: Intelligent and social animals who deserve to be treated with dignity and respect

values.statement: Every rabbit has intrinsic worth regardless of breed, temperament, health, or age
values.hrs_quote: Valuable as individuals, regardless of breed purity, temperament, state of health
values.rorg_quote: Intrinsic worth as an individual, regardless of breed purity, temperament, state of health

values.statement: Domestic rabbits are human-dependent; their care is our responsibility
values.hrs_quote: Product of human interference — human responsibility that these animals be cared for in a manner appropriate to their needs
values.rorg_quote: Rely on humans for their well-being, making it our duty

values.statement: Spay/neuter is in the best interest of all domestic rabbits
values.hrs_quote: In the best interest of domesticated rabbits to be neutered/spayed
values.rorg_quote: Neutering or spaying domestic rabbits is in their best interest

values.statement: Regular veterinary care is essential for domestic rabbits
values.rorg_quote: Regular veterinary treatment and care are essential for the overall health and happiness

values.statement: Rabbits deserve the same rights, care, and opportunity for longevity as dogs and cats
values.hrs_quote: Same individual rights, level of care, and opportunity for longevity as dogs and cats

values.statement: Rabbits require mental stimulation, exercise, enrichment, and social interaction
values.hrs_quote: Mental stimulation, toys, exercise, environmental activity, and social interaction
values.rorg_quote: Important role of mental stimulation, playtime, exercise, environmental activity, and social interaction

values.statement: The welfare of all rabbits is the primary concern; exploitation in all forms is opposed
values.hrs_quote: Welfare of all rabbits is our primary consideration. We are against the exploitation of rabbits in any and all forms
values.rorg_quote: Against the exploitation of rabbits in any and all forms unless it directly benefits the rabbits under the guidance of a rabbit rescue

values.statement: Public education about rabbit care is core to the mission
values.hrs_quote: Change cultural perceptions of domesticated rabbits and improve their quality of care and living standards
values.rorg_quote: Dedicated to working toward a future where all domestic rabbits are given the care and attention they deserve

values.statement: Positive reinforcement only; fear-based and punishment-based methods must be avoided
values.rorg_quote: Encourage the use of positive reinforcement techniques — imperative to discourage and avoid fear-based and punishment-based behavior-modification techniques

values.statement: No support for or alignment with groups that promote rabbits as food animals
values.hrs_quote: We do not support or align ourself with any group or individual promoting rabbits as food animals

values.statement: Wild rabbit populations benefit most from minimal human intervention, except in rehabilitation situations
values.hrs_quote: In the best interest of wild rabbits that human intervention be held to a minimum
values.rorg_quote: In the best interest of wild rabbits that human intervention be minimized — exceptions include rehabilitation or endangered species situations

closed_organizations.name: North Florida Rabbit Rescue
closed_organizations.reason: Permanently closed due to medical concerns

excluded_organizations.name: Bunnychick Haven of Navarre
excluded_organizations.reason: Multi-species petting zoo and wellness retreat (rabbits, chickens, goats)

excluded_organizations.name: Pawphanage for Wayward Pets
excluded_organizations.reason: Multi-animal rescue (cats, dogs, hamsters, guinea pigs, rabbits) — not rabbit-focused; very small scale with minimal rabbit inventory

excluded_organizations.name: Live Freely Sanctuary
excluded_organizations.reason: Multi-animal sanctuary focused on animals with disabilities — not rabbit-specific; questionable activity level (Sunbiz lapsed and reinstated, $0 reported assets/income, no website)

excluded_organizations.name: Palm Beach Rabbit, Guinea Pig, etc. Rescue
excluded_organizations.reason: Not a standalone organization — operates as a program of Palm Beach Parrot & Bird Rescue Inc.; multi-animal (rabbits, guinea pigs, small animals alongside birds); no dedicated website

excluded_organizations.name: Floppy Ear Rescue & Sanctuary
excluded_organizations.reason: Multi-animal rescue (rabbits, chinchillas, guinea pigs, reptiles); currently not accepting new surrenders due to limited space and resources

excluded_organizations.name: CudaBear's Lair Animal Sanctuary
excluded_organizations.reason: Farm and animal sanctuary — primarily pigs, goats, horses, emus, reptiles, and cats; not rabbit-focused

excluded_organizations.name: Farmhouse Animal & Nature Sanctuary
excluded_organizations.reason: Multi-species sanctuary in Myakka City, FL — exotic and farm animals including rabbits; 501(c)(3) nonprofit

excluded_organizations.name: H&S Homestead Exotic and Livestock Rescue
excluded_organizations.reason: Exotic and livestock rescue in LaBelle, FL — 501(c)(3) nonprofit since 2023; rescuing since 2020

excluded_organizations.name: Redemptive Love Farm & Rescue
excluded_organizations.reason: Farm sanctuary and rescue in Tallahassee, FL — 100+ animals; 501(c)(3) nonprofit; rehomes over 200 rescue animals per year

excluded_organizations.name: Saving Sage Animal Rescue Foundation
excluded_organizations.reason: Multi-animal rescue in Lauderhill, FL — 501(c)(3) nonprofit since 2014; 30,000+ animals saved

excluded_organizations.name: The Runaways Animal Rescue
excluded_organizations.reason: Multi-animal rescue in Port Richey, FL — 501(c)(3) nonprofit; 7,000 sq ft facility in Pasco County

excluded_organizations.name: Bunny Boss
excluded_organizations.reason: Small animal rescue in DeLand, FL — offers rescue alongside other services; no Florida corporate filing or 501(c)(3) on record

organizations.name: Gainesville Rabbit Rescue
organizations.service_area: Statewide via foster network; primary location North Central FL
organizations.mission_statement: To rescue, rehabilitate, and adopt out rabbits to loving homes, as well as educate current and potential rabbit owners. GRR does not turn away any rabbit regardless of disability, illness, age, or temperament.

board.status_label: Board confirmed — names not publicly listed
board.notes: Long-established 501(c)(3); board existence required by FL law. Individual names not published on website or GuideStar.

organizations.name: Orlando Rabbit Care & Adoptions
organizations.mission_statement: An organization dedicated to the health, care and enjoyment of house rabbits as family members.

nonprofit.guidestar_note: Highest GuideStar transparency rating on this list

board.notes: Named board members and roles — including Secretary of the Board — are listed on the About Us page at orlandorabbit.org.

organizations.name: Tampa Bay House Rabbit Rescue
organizations.service_area: All of Florida; primarily West Coast; 100% foster-based
organizations.mission_statement: To promote knowledgeable and correct care of domestic house rabbits, including helping the public gain this knowledge; rescuing and rehabilitating domestic rabbits; aiding other rescues and shelters with rabbit overpopulation; assisting other rabbit welfare organizations; and promoting fostering and adoption of domestic house rabbits, especially in the State of Florida.

board.notes: Three named board members listed on tbhrr.org/about-us/board-of-directors/: President (Licensed HRS Educator), Secretary (Co-Founder), and Vice President.

organizations.hrs_connection: Licensed HRS Educator on Board of Directors; explicitly states alliance with House Rabbit Society

organizations.name: Southwest Florida House Rabbit Rescue
organizations.service_area: SW Florida (Lee and Collier counties primarily); foster-based network
organizations.mission_statement: To save the lives of domestic rabbits through outstanding education, advocacy work on their behalf, and providing excellent adoptions. We strive to elevate the respect of rabbits in our society to that of other companion animals.

board.notes: Six named board members and roles listed on the About page at respectforrabbits.org: President, Vice-President, Treasurer, Secretary, Director, and Medical Director (DVM).

organizations.name: East Coast Rabbit Rescue
organizations.service_area: Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade Counties
organizations.mission_statement: To rescue rabbits from abandoned situations, euthanasia list, private surrendering and hoarding cases. We will ensure the best care, shelter and love for each animal. We will provide necessary veterinary care including spay/neuter surgery, microchip, RHDV2 vaccine and eventually find the perfect forever homes.
organizations.founded_source: FL Division of Corporations — filed January 25, 2016

board.notes: GuideStar explicitly notes 'no recorded board members.' Website does not list board. FL corporate records show 3 active principals as required by law.

organizations.name: Penny & Wild Smalls of South Florida
organizations.service_area: Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach Counties; 100% foster-based
organizations.mission_statement: To rescue rabbits and guinea pigs who have been abandoned outdoors, are running out of time in shelters, or need complex medical care. We also provide free education to the community on proper small animal care and accept owner surrenders on a case by case basis. Not all rabbits and guinea pigs that we encounter are adoptable, due to age or medical conditions, and for those who are not we provide lifelong sanctuary to ensure they live out their lives in comfort and safety.
organizations.founded_source: Estimated from EIN registration data; exact founding date not publicly stated

board.notes: No board listing found on website or in public filings.

organizations.species_note: Also rescues guinea pigs in addition to rabbits

organizations.name: Space Coast Bunnies, Inc.
organizations.website_note: No dedicated website. Primary online presence via Linktree, Facebook, Instagram, and Petfinder.
organizations.mission_statement: A 501(c)(3) helping buns in need find safe, loving, indoor homes. Rabbit rescue, advocacy and education organization in Brevard County, Florida.
organizations.founded_source: OpenCorporates FL record — company number N20000006718

board.notes: No dedicated website; no board information available publicly. No listing on GuideStar governance section.

organizations.name: Bebette's Bunny Rescue
organizations.service_area: NE Florida and Jacksonville metro region as well as the Tallahassee area
organizations.mission_statement: To save rabbit lives through collective efforts. By promoting adoption, spreading awareness, educating the public, and providing support to shelters and rescues, we aim to ensure a brighter and safer future for these furry companions.
organizations.founded_source: bebettesbunnyrescue.org/faq — 'started in January of 2022'; bebettesbunnyrescue.org/ourstory — '501(c)3 status in January 2022'

board.notes: Florida Division of Corporations records (search.sunbiz.org) show 3 active principals — the legal minimum. Names verifiable via public record. Not listed on their website.

organizations.name: Holly Hops Rabbit Rescue & Rehabilitation
organizations.mission_statement: To rescue, rehabilitate, and rehome domestic rabbits that have been left to fend for themselves in dangerous situations or surrendered by people who can no longer care for them. We also educate the public on indoor domestic rabbit care that benefits the rabbit and provides a safe living environment.

nonprofit.status_label: Confirmed via GuideStar — not stated on website
nonprofit.guidestar_note: Confirmed 501(c)(3) via GuideStar profile. Organization does not explicitly state 501(c)(3) status on their own website.

organizations.founded_source: FL Division of Corporations — incorporated 2023; Sunbiz record N230000127190

board.notes: No board information on website or public filings. Note: organization also advocates for guinea pig education; rescue operations appear to be rabbit-focused.

organizations.species_note: Rescue is rabbit-focused; website includes guinea pig education as a secondary interest

organizations.name: Southeast FL Rabbit Rescue
organizations.mission_statement: Southeast FL Rabbit Rescue operates as a 100% foster-based network of volunteers, dedicated to the rescue and responsible re-homing of rabbits and guinea pigs. We focus on saving rabbits and guinea pigs that have been abandoned and those at risk in shelters, providing essential veterinary care, spaying/neutering, and promoting their well-being through education and advocacy.

nonprofit.guidestar_note: No GuideStar profile found as of March 2026 — too new to have filed a 990-N yet.

organizations.founded_source: FL Sunbiz record — effective date 09/20/2024. IRS 501(c)(3) exemption effective same date.

board.notes: No board information on website or in any public filing. Organization is less than one year old.

organizations.species_note: Rescues both rabbits and guinea pigs
organizations.directory_note: Newest organization in the directory. 501(c)(3) confirmed via IRS determination letter (11/19/2024). Listed as of March 2026 with the caveat that this org has under one year of operation — outreach and annual re-verification recommended.

organizations.name: Fort Wilbur Rabbit Resources
organizations.service_area: Statewide via foster network; free grooming in Volusia, Flagler, and Lake Counties; also assists shelters in Georgia and the Carolinas
organizations.website_note: Primary Wix site; fortwilbur.org may have intermittent issues
organizations.mission_statement: A 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to rehabilitating and rehoming critically neglected and abused rabbits, with a broader mission of rabbit rehabilitation, therapy, and education. The only rabbit rescue in the United States that offers all of its programs free of charge, including grooming, health evaluations, workshops, one-on-one training, a 24-hour phone line, and disaster relief.
organizations.founded_source: Started informally in 2011; incorporated as FL nonprofit July 2019; IRS 501(c)(3) effective September 2022

board.notes: CEO: Vanessa A. Chaves (founder), COO: Desiree Razon, CFO/Secretary: Adam W. Hill. ~12 consistent volunteers. Annual report filed April 2025.

caveats: Only accepts rabbits from other 501(c)(3) rescues and state shelters — does not take surrenders from the general public
Formerly known as Fort Wilbur Rabbit Rescue — renamed to Fort Wilbur Rabbit Resources in January 2024

organizations.name: Marley's Carrot Patch Rabbit Rescue
organizations.mission_statement: Rescue of rabbits and other small animals, adoption of adoptable rabbits and small animals, sanctuary and perpetual care of non-adoptable animals, and education of rabbit and small animal care.
organizations.mission_source: Charity Navigator / eBay for Charity profile
organizations.founded_source: Sunbiz filing date 06/30/2015; IRS ruling year 2016

organizations.name: Houserabbit Adoption, Rescue and Education
organizations.mission_statement: To rescue and rehabilitate injured, neglected, and abandoned domestic rabbits. Advocates for spaying/neutering all domestic rabbits and caring for them indoors as family members. Provides permanent sanctuary for rabbits with disabilities and care for unreleasable wild lagomorphs.
organizations.founded_source: Sunbiz filing date 12/10/2004; IRS tax-exempt since November 2006
caveats: Affiliated with the University of Miami
Current activity level not yet confirmed

organizations.name: Bugsy's Rabbit Sanctuary
organizations.mission_statement: To rescue, rehabilitate, and rehome domestic rabbits, and to provide education through community outreach.

nonprofit.ein_note: No EIN found in public databases

board.notes: No Florida corporate filing on record

caveats: No Florida corporate filing or 501(c)(3) status on record
Currently on hiatus — not accepting new surrenders. Still active as a rabbit education and referral resource, connecting those looking to surrender or adopt with other local rescues.

organizations.name: Happy Bunny Rescue

contact.contact_note: No website or social media presence found

caveats: Sunbiz filing is inactive — admin dissolution for annual report (September 2024)
No website or active social media presence found
